ideas.title: ARRL Field Day Is Almost Here — Your Practical Prep Guide
ideas.why_now: Field Day falls June 28-29 and operators are already practicing CW sims and antenna builds for it this week.
ideas.angle: Walk everyday hams through low-stress Field Day prep: picking a category, simple antenna options, logging basics, and how to show up to a club site and actually have fun without feeling lost.

sources.label: Mastodon - Field Day CW practice discussion

ideas.title: CQ Pride On the Air: Special Event Stations and Why They Matter
ideas.why_now: CQ Pride 2026 just wrapped June 15 with special callsigns N6L/N6G/N6B/N6T active, generating fresh on-air buzz.
ideas.angle: Use CQ Pride as a springboard to explain how special event and themed station activations work, how to chase them, and why inclusive on-air events are good for the hobby's growth.

sources.label: Mastodon - CQ Pride callsigns active now

ideas.title: Hawaii Declares June Amateur Radio Month — What It Means for the Hobby
ideas.why_now: Hawaii officially declared June 2026 as Amateur Radio Month, a timely hook for celebrating the hobby publicly.
ideas.angle: Celebrate with a fun episode on how hams can promote the hobby in their own communities — demo events, youth outreach, and simple ways to be a good ambassador without being preachy.

sources.label: QRZ Forums - Hawaii Declares June 2026 as Amateur Radio Month
QRZ Forums - Young operator makes 60 contacts during W1AW/4 special event

ideas.title: POTA by Ferry: Taking Your Radio Somewhere Genuinely Adventurous
ideas.why_now: An Ireland's Eye island activation by ferry is planned for June 21, and Faroe Islands SOTA/POTA combos posted this week show the trend is hot.
ideas.angle: Inspire activators to think beyond roadside parks — cover the logistics of boat, ferry, and island activations: gear protection, power, operating time windows, and why it's worth the extra effort.

sources.label: Mastodon - POTA adventure on the high seas, Ireland's Eye
YouTube - POTA Tips I Wish I Knew Sooner by Conor KF8CNK

ideas.title: SteppIR Is Back: What Returning Gear Brands Mean for Ham Radio
ideas.why_now: SteppIR re-entering the ham antenna market is generating forum discussion and excitement among HF operators right now.
ideas.angle: Use SteppIR's return as a jumping-off point to discuss what makes a great HF antenna worth the investment, and how everyday hams should evaluate gear from brands making a comeback.

sources.label: QRZ Forums - Steppir getting back into the ham antenna business

ideas.title: Did FT8 Kill Ham Radio — Or Save It? Let's Actually Talk About It
ideas.why_now: A QRZ thread titled 'FT8 Killed The Radio Ham' is circulating alongside a broader 'What Happened To Ham Radio?' thread this week.
ideas.angle: Give a balanced, non-elitist take: FT8 gets people on HF who'd otherwise never try it, and that's worth defending — while also exploring how to layer in more 'human' operating once you're hooked.

sources.label: QRZ Forums - FT8 Killed The Radio Ham
QRZ Forums - What Happened To Ham Radio?
Mastodon - speaker wire 6m dipole FT8 success story

ideas.title: Running Comms at a Real-World Event: An After-Action Report
ideas.why_now: A ham just posted a detailed after-action report from providing communications at a teacher march in Raleigh using PTToC radios.
ideas.angle: Walk listeners through what real event comms looks like in 2026 — from traditional simplex/repeater setups to modern PTToC options — so any ham can volunteer confidently at their next local event.

sources.label: Mastodon - after-action report running comms at teacher march, Raleigh
QRZ Forums - Amateur Radio Net Control Best Practices

ideas.title: Open Source QRM Hunting: Using the N6OL Powerline Monitor
ideas.why_now: The N6OL Powerline QRM Monitor was just released as open source on GitHub this week, giving every ham a new free tool.
ideas.angle: Demystify powerline QRM for everyday operators: what it sounds like, how it ruins HF fun, and how to use this new open-source tool to identify and document interference sources in your neighborhood.

sources.label: Mastodon - N6OL Powerline QRM Monitor now open source
